meta.title: Toolbar
meta.description: Action toolbar for the OSD Viewer — Side, Bottom, and single toolbar buttons grouped by purpose.

hero.title: Toolbar
hero.desc: Toolbar is the component that surfaces the actions needed to complete a task.

usage.title: Usage
usage.desc:
Toolbar is used in the Worker OSD Viewer, in two main placements:

• Side Toolbar
• Bottom Toolbar

A single Toolbar Button can also be used standalone.

positioning.title: Positioning
positioning.desc: Toolbar sits above the working section. Place it where it doesn't interfere with the work — top, bottom, or side.

components.title: Components
components.desc: Toolbar surfaces the actions needed to complete a task.

side.title: Side Toolbar
side.desc: Vertical form, anchored to the left or right edge of the host workspace. A Divider can separate elements by purpose.

bottom.title: Bottom Toolbar
bottom.desc: Horizontal form, anchored to the bottom of the host workspace. Functionally distinct groups separate with an 8px gap.

single.title: Single Toolbar Button
single.desc: Unlike Side and Bottom Toolbar groups, this element runs standalone. Roles and types vary widely.

extra.title: Extra Case
extra.desc: Toolbar can adapt to different purposes in the workspace.

state.title: State
state.desc: Toolbar Button has 5 states — Default, Hover, Selected, Select Hover, Disabled. Selected and Select Hover may be omitted depending on intent.

hover.title: Hover
hover.desc: Hovering over a Toolbar element should surface a Tooltip describing the tool. Omission is permitted by context.

selected.title: Selected & Clicked
selected.desc: When a specific tool requires a separate component in Selected state, position it using the Tooltip placement convention (4px). Unlike Selected, when a Clicked action surfaces a separate component (no dedicated Clicked Toolbar Button state defined), use an 8px gap.

clicked.title: Clicked & Change
clicked.desc: Bottom Toolbar buttons typically don't change visually on click — they just execute. Exception: Show / Hide directly reflects the current Annotation Object state in the Viewer for an intuitive read.

rows.state: Default, Hover, Selected, Select Hover, Disabled
Default, Hover, Selected, Select Hover, Disabled
Default, Hover, Selected, Select Hover, Disabled
Default, Hover, Selected, Select Hover, Disabled
Default, Hover, Selected, Select Hover, Disabled

tooltip.title: Tooltip
tooltip.desc:
Icon-based Toolbar tools surface their description through a Tooltip. The Tooltip typically holds name, description, and keyboard shortcut. Self-evident tools can omit description; tools that don't need a shortcut within the workspace can omit shortcut info.

Tooltip behavior follows the standard Tooltip policy — surfaces on Hover.

cursor.title: Cursor
cursor.desc: Toolbar elements expose a wide range of behaviors. The cursor shape shifts based on the function each Toolbar Button activates.
